Safety devices — anti-two-block, boom angle indicator, load moment indicator, outrigger interlocks
Wire rope — visual inspection for damage, kinks, bird-caging
Hooks — safety latches, no cracks or deformation
Load chart in cab and readable
All shift inspection items in detail
Structural components for deformation, cracks, corrosion
Wire rope condition (measure diameter, check for broken wires — 6 randomly in one lay or 3 in one strand = replacement)
monthly.documentation: Written record signed by inspector with date and crane identification

annual_comprehensive.by: Competent person (many sites require third-party qualified inspector)
items: NDT of critical welds and structural members if applicable
Load test if required by manufacturer or after major repair
All safety device calibration and testing
Review of maintenance and repair records
annual_comprehensive.documentation: Written report with date, inspector qualifications, serial number, findings, and certification of compliance

items: No visible damage, corrosion, or leakage

6_year.applies_to: Stored pressure extinguishers (dry chemical, clean agent)

items: Pressure test of cylinder to verify structural integrity

check_for: Cracks, dents, or deformation in shell
Frayed, torn, or damaged suspension
Fading or chalking (UV degradation)
Stickers covering damage (only allowed per manufacturer guidelines)
Manufacturing date (replace per manufacturer guidance, typically 2-5 years for shell, 1 year for suspension)

safety_harness_fall_protection.frequency: Before each use by user; formally by competent person per manufacturer recommendations (annually minimum)
check_for: Frayed, cut, or worn webbing
Cracked, bent, or broken buckles and D-rings
Damaged or missing labels/tags (if label is missing or illegible, remove from service)
Stitching — broken, pulled, or cut threads
Evidence of chemical damage, burns, or heat exposure
Evidence of impact loading (stretched, deformed) — remove from service after any fall arrest event
Inspect all connection points (lanyard to D-ring, carabiner function, SRL retraction)
safety_harness_fall_protection.retirement: Per manufacturer guidelines. Immediately after fall arrest event. When any defect is found. Typical maximum service life: 5-10 years from date of first use (varies by manufacturer).

check_for: ANSI Z87.1 marking on lens and frame
Scratched or pitted lenses (impair vision)
Bent or loose frames
Missing or damaged side shields
Facepiece — cracks, tears, distortion
Head straps — elasticity, cracks, breaks
Inhalation/exhalation valves — function, damage
Filter/cartridge — proper type, expiration, saturation
Seal — positive and negative pressure user seal check each donning

requirements: Walking paths clear of debris, tools, materials, cords
No tripping hazards (cover or mark floor openings, secure cords/hoses)
Waste containers available and not overflowing
Scrap materials removed from work area regularly
Flammable waste in approved containers with lids
Material stacked and stored safely (not above head height unless on rated storage)
Tools returned to proper storage when not in use
Adequate lighting in work areas and walkways
Stairways and access points clear
No eating or drinking in work areas where chemicals present
Spills cleaned up immediately
Laydown areas organized with designated storage locations
housekeeping_standards.frequency_of_inspection: Continuous (every worker's responsibility). Formal inspection daily by supervisor. Weekly by safety.

required_content: New action items with responsible parties

safety_meeting_minutes.retention: Duration of project plus minimum 3 years. Many companies retain for 5+ years.

required_elements: Employee name and unique identifier (badge number, SSN last 4)
Certification number and expiration date if applicable

specific_osha_required_records.fall_protection: Written certification of training (name, date, trainer signature) per 1926.503
specific_osha_required_records.respiratory_protection: Fit test records, medical clearance, training per 1910.134

training_records.retention: Duration of employment. Medical/exposure records: employment + 30 years.

osha_form_301.title: Injury and Illness Incident Report
osha_form_301.due: Within 7 calendar days of learning of the injury/illness
content: Case details (date, time, location, what happened, what object/substance, what was the injury/illness, what body part)
osha_form_301.retention: 5 years following the year the record covers

osha_form_300.title: Log of Work-Related Injuries and Illnesses
osha_form_300.when_required: Running log maintained throughout the calendar year
content: Case number, employee name, job title, date of injury, where event occurred, description, classification (death, days away, restricted, other recordable), whether injury or illness
osha_form_300.retention: 5 years following the year the record covers
osha_form_300.privacy_cases: Employee name withheld for: sexual assault, HIV/hepatitis, needle-stick, tuberculosis, mental illness, musculoskeletal disorders (not on construction typically)

osha_form_300A.title: Summary of Work-Related Injuries and Illnesses
osha_form_300A.posting_period: February 1 through April 30 of the following year
content: Total number of cases by category
Total days away from work and restricted days
osha_form_300A.certification: Must be certified by company executive (owner, officer, highest-ranking official at the establishment)
osha_form_300A.electronic_reporting: Establishments with 250+ employees, or in high-hazard industries with 20+ employees, must electronically submit 300A data to OSHA annually via ITA (Injury Tracking Application)

recommended_elements: Date, time, specific location of incident
Injured person information (name, employer, trade, experience)
Environmental conditions at time of incident
Root cause analysis (method used and findings)
Long-term corrective actions (with responsible party and due date)

safety_observation_cards.purpose: Document field observations of safe and at-risk behaviors/conditions
safety_observation_cards.tracking: Entered into database for trending and analysis. Target completion rate tracked as leading indicator.

content: Status (open, in progress, completed, verified, closed)

permit_archives.retention_recommendation: Minimum duration of project plus 3 years. Hot work permits: 1 year minimum. Confined space permits: 1 year minimum per OSHA. Some companies retain all permits for 5+ years.
permit_archives.storage: Organized by date and area. Accessible for audit and investigation purposes.

types_and_retention.crane_inspections: Monthly and annual documented inspections retained for duration of crane on project plus 3 years
types_and_retention.scaffold_inspections: Daily tag records retained for duration of scaffold on project
types_and_retention.harness_inspection: Per manufacturer — annual formal inspection records retained for life of equipment
types_and_retention.fire_extinguisher: Monthly inspection tags on unit. Annual maintenance records retained per NFPA 10.
types_and_retention.rigging_and_slings: Inspection before each use. Annual inspection by competent person. Records retained for life of equipment.
types_and_retention.electrical_equipment: GFCI testing records, ground fault testing (assured grounding program). Retained for duration of project.

gas_monitors.bump_test: Before each use (expose to known concentration to verify sensor response). Document date, time, result, instrument ID.
gas_monitors.full_calibration: Per manufacturer recommendation (typically monthly or when bump test fails). Document date, gas concentrations used, instrument ID, calibrator ID, results, technician.
gas_monitors.sensor_replacement: Per manufacturer lifespan (varies by sensor type: O2 sensors 1-2 years, LEL sensors 2-3 years, H2S sensors 2-3 years)

noise_monitoring_equipment.calibration: Before and after each use with calibrator. Annual laboratory calibration.
noise_monitoring_equipment.documentation: Pre/post calibration results, instrument serial number, date, operator

fall_protection_equipment.SRL_retractables: Annual inspection by competent person or manufacturer-authorized technician. Documented with serial number, date, findings, technician certification.

section_12_common_violations.title: Common Safety Violations on Industrial Construction Sites

top_10_osha_citations_construction.note: Based on OSHA published data for fiscal year 2023/2024. Rankings may shift slightly year to year but these consistently appear in top 10.

citations.standard: 1926.501 — Fall Protection (Duty to Have Fall Protection)
citations.description: Failure to provide fall protection for workers at heights of 6 feet or more
common_specific_violations: Workers on scaffolds, roofs, or leading edges without harness/guardrail
Unprotected floor holes or wall openings
Inadequate or missing guardrails on platforms
Workers in aerial lifts without harness/lanyard attached

citations.description: Improper ladder use, condition, or setup
common_specific_violations: Ladder not extending 3 feet above landing surface
Ladder not secured at top
Using damaged or defective ladders
Carrying items while climbing (loss of 3-point contact)
Standing on top 2 rungs of step ladder

citations.description: Scaffold construction, inspection, or use deficiencies
common_specific_violations: Missing guardrails, midrails, or toeboards
Scaffold not fully planked (gaps over 1 inch)
Scaffold not inspected by competent person
Exceeding load capacity
Scaffold not tied to structure when height exceeds 4:1 ratio
Workers using cross braces as access

citations.description: Failure to communicate chemical hazards to workers
common_specific_violations: Missing or incomplete Safety Data Sheets
Workers not trained on chemical hazards
Containers not labeled or improperly labeled
Written HazCom program missing or inadequate

citations.description: Failure to train workers on fall hazards and protection systems
common_specific_violations: No documented fall protection training
Workers not trained on specific equipment being used
Retraining not conducted after changes

citations.description: Fall protection equipment not meeting OSHA specifications
common_specific_violations: Guardrail height incorrect (not 42 inches +/- 3 inches)
Anchor points not rated for 5,000 lbs
Using body belts instead of full body harness for fall arrest
Free fall distance exceeding 6 feet
No protective system for excavations over 5 feet
No competent person inspecting excavation
No ladder/egress within 25 feet lateral travel
Spoil pile within 2 feet of excavation edge
Utilities not located before digging
No written respiratory protection program
Workers not fit-tested
Facial hair interfering with seal
Wrong respirator type for the hazard
Medical evaluation not completed before use
Missing stair rails or handrails
Stairways not provided when break in elevation is 19 inches or more
No harness/lanyard in boom lift
Untrained operators
Exceeding rated capacity
Operating on unstable ground
Using lift as crane (lifting materials not designed for)

most_common_near_misses: Near-contact with overhead power lines (cranes, aerial lifts, boom trucks)
Near-miss caught-in/between (moving equipment parts, closing doors/panels)
Near-miss falls from height (misstep on scaffold, ladder slip)

focus_four.description: OSHA's Focus Four hazards account for approximately 60%+ of construction fatalities

common_scenarios: Caught in or between equipment parts
Caught between equipment and fixed objects

other_significant_causes: Fires and explosions (especially in refinery/petrochemical construction)
Transportation incidents (driving to/from site, equipment transport)

primary_hazards: Electrocution/shock (contact with energized conductors)
Arc flash/arc blast (NFPA 70E categories)
Falls (work at height for cable tray, conduit, overhead installations)
Struck by (conduit, cable reels)
Burns (from energized equipment, soldering)
Confined space (electrical vaults, manholes, duct banks)
key_violations: Failure to lock out/tag out (most critical)
Working energized without proper PPE or permit
Lack of GFCI protection on temporary circuits
Damaged extension cords/tools still in use
Inadequate arc flash labeling

primary_hazards: Burns (welding, cutting, hot metal, steam)
Fire/explosion (hot work near flammables, line breaks on process piping)
Fume inhalation (welding fumes, galvanized, stainless, chromium, manganese)
Caught between/struck by (pipe handling, rigging, equipment)
Falls (work at height for elevated piping systems)
Confined space (vessels, tanks, large diameter pipe)
Noise (grinding, air arc gouging, pneumatic tools)
Eye injury (flash burn, grinding debris, chemical splash during line breaks)
key_violations: Hot work permit deficiencies or missing fire watch
Inadequate fume ventilation/extraction
Missing or improper fire blanket/shield placement
Failure to verify line contents before line break
Inadequate rigging for pipe lifts

primary_hazards: Falls from height (leading cause of ironworker fatalities)
Struck by (falling steel, swinging loads, bolts/tools)
Caught between (steel members, crane loads and structure)
Crane-related (overloading, rigging failure, power line contact)
Lacerations (sharp edges, burrs on steel)
Overexertion (heavy material handling)
Burns (welding connections, torch cutting)
key_violations: Fall protection deficiencies (connectors at height without protection)
Inadequate bolting before releasing crane load (minimum 2 bolts per connection)
Lack of tag lines on suspended loads
Improper rigging (incorrect sling angles, overloaded slings)
Workers in crane swing radius

primary_hazards: Electrical shock (energized circuits, testing, calibration)
Chemical exposure (process connections, tubing breaks, sample points)
Falls (elevated instrumentation on columns, vessels, pipe racks)
Confined space (instrument installations inside vessels)
H2S/toxic gas exposure (working on analyzers, sample systems in process areas)
Burns (tubing/process connections on hot lines, steam)
key_violations: Working on energized instrumentation circuits without proper LOTO
Failure to verify process isolation before breaking instrument connections
Missing confined space permit for vessel entry instrument work
Inadequate 4-gas monitoring in process areas
Not wearing proper PPE for line break on process connections

voice_assistant_quick_reference.title: Quick Reference for Voice Assistant Responses
voice_assistant_quick_reference.description: Key numbers and thresholds that safety officers frequently need to recall quickly

critical_numbers.fall_protection_trigger_height: 6 feet (construction), 4 feet (general industry), 15 feet (steel erection connectors)
critical_numbers.excavation_protective_system: 5 feet depth triggers protective system requirement
critical_numbers.excavation_egress: Ladder within 25 feet lateral travel for 4+ foot depth
critical_numbers.fire_watch_duration: Minimum 30 minutes after hot work (60 minutes on some sites)
